name: Progressive Web App (PWA) Patterns
description: Comprehensive guide to building progressive web apps with optimal UX patterns and best practices
definition: Progressive Web Apps are web applications that use modern web capabilities to deliver app-like experiences to users. They work for every user, regardless of browser, are responsive, connectivity-independent, fresh, safe, discoverable, re-engageable, installable, and linkable.

core_characteristics.progressive: Work for every user, regardless of browser choice (progressive enhancement)
core_characteristics.responsive: Fit any form factor: desktop, mobile, tablet, or future devices
core_characteristics.connectivity_independent: Work offline or on low-quality networks (service workers)
core_characteristics.app_like: Feel like an app with app-style interactions and navigation
core_characteristics.safe: Served via HTTPS to prevent snooping and ensure content integrity
core_characteristics.discoverable: Identifiable as 'applications' via web app manifest
core_characteristics.re_engageable: Make re-engagement easy through push notifications
core_characteristics.installable: Allow users to 'add to home screen' without app store
core_characteristics.linkable: Share via URL, don't require complex installation

service_workers.description: JavaScript that runs in the background, separate from web page

register.when: On page load (after window.load to not block rendering)

activate.event: After install, when SW takes control

fetch.use: Intercept requests, serve from cache or network

cache_first.description: Serve from cache, fallback to network

stale_while_revalidate.description: Serve from cache, update cache in background
stale_while_revalidate.use_when: Balance freshness and speed (images, non-critical data)
stale_while_revalidate.pattern: Cache response AND fetch to update cache

update_pattern.description: How to handle service worker updates

web_app_manifest.description: JSON file that describes the app (name, icons, display mode)

short_name.description: Short name for home screen (12 chars max)

options.standalone: Looks like native app (recommended for most PWAs)

theme_color.description: UI chrome color (address bar on mobile)

description.example: A fast and reliable way to...

maskable_icons.description: Icons that adapt to different shapes (Android adaptive icons)
maskable_icons.safe_zone: 80% of icon in center (20% may be masked)

https_requirement.description: PWAs must be served over HTTPS
https_requirement.why: Service workers have powerful capabilities (intercept network requests)

app_shell_pattern.description: Cache minimal HTML/CSS/JS to display app UI, load content dynamically
benefits: Works offline (shows shell even without content)
app_shell_pattern.implementation: Cache shell assets in service worker install event

offline_fallback.description: Show meaningful offline page instead of browser's default

patterns: Disable actions that require network (dim buttons)

best_practices: Don't just show 'No connection' (offer cached content)
Make it clear what's available offline
Use background sync to sync when back online
Show sync status (syncing, synced, failed)

add_to_home_screen.description: Prompt users to install PWA to home screen

criteria.automatic: Browser shows prompt if criteria met (HTTPS, manifest, service worker, engagement)
criteria.manual: You can defer and show custom prompt

examples.good: Install for faster access and offline use

permission_flow.2_ask: Request permission only after user interaction
permission_flow.3_respect: If denied, don't ask again (browser blocks)

best_practices: Never ask for permission on page load
Explain why notifications are valuable
Provide notification settings (frequency, types)
Allow users to disable notifications
Make notifications relevant and timely
Don't spam (reduce frequency if not engaging)

body.example: Hey, are you free for coffee?

tag.use: Replace previous notifications with same tag

background_sync.description: Defer actions until stable connection (send message, post form)

app_updates.description: How to handle PWA updates gracefully

silent_update.description: Update in background, apply on next load

app_updates.best_practice: Prompt user, but don't force. Let them choose when to update.

precaching.description: Cache critical assets during service worker install

runtime_caching.strategies: Use appropriate caching strategy per resource type
runtime_caching.example: Images: stale-while-revalidate, API: network-first, Static: cache-first

criteria.manifest: Valid web app manifest with name, icons, start_url, display
criteria.service_worker: Registered service worker with fetch event handler
criteria.engagement: User has visited site at least once (Chrome: 30 seconds)

browser_support.safari_ios: Limited (no beforeinstallprompt, manual 'Add to Home Screen')

ios_specific.install: User must manually tap Share → Add to Home Screen

workbox.description: Google's library for service worker development

pwa_builder.description: Tool to package PWA for app stores

install_testing.chrome: DevTools → Application → Manifest → 'Add to home screen'
install_testing.ios: Safari → Share → Add to Home Screen

update_testing.method: Change service worker, reload with DevTools 'Update on reload' checked

best_practices: Serve over HTTPS (required for service workers)
Create valid web app manifest with all required fields
Register service worker after page load (don't block rendering)
Cache app shell for fast, offline-capable UI
Use appropriate caching strategies per resource type
Provide offline fallback page
Show meaningful offline UX (not just 'You are offline')
Don't ask for notification permission immediately
Defer install prompt, show at contextual moment
Update service worker gracefully (prompt user, don't force)
Test offline functionality thoroughly
Optimize performance (lazy load, code split, compress)
Use Workbox for easier service worker development
Provide 192x192 and 512x512 icons (and maskable)
Test on real devices (especially iOS for limitations)
Monitor Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)
Use background sync for offline actions
Show sync status (pending, synced, failed)
Make notifications timely and relevant
Provide notification preferences
anti_patterns: Asking for notification permission on page load
Forcing install prompt immediately
Not providing offline functionality (defeats PWA purpose)
Caching too much (wastes user's storage)
Not handling service worker updates (users stuck on old version)
Using 'cache-only' strategy for dynamic content
Not testing offline experience
Ignoring iOS limitations (assuming all features work)
Not providing meaningful offline fallback
Forcing updates (may lose user data)
Showing generic 'No internet' message (offer cached content)
Not versioning caches (old caches pile up)
Blocking rendering with service worker registration
Not using HTTPS (service workers won't work)
Missing required manifest fields
Not testing on real devices
Spamming push notifications
Not explaining notification value before asking permission
Not providing way to disable notifications
Assuming PWA = native app (it's still web with limitations)
resources: Can I Use - Service Worker browser support
